todays_thought: On March 19, 2026, Bitcoin dipped to $69,678. Extreme fear dominates — the capitulation phase tests the faithful. Today, the world is discussing: "5 Ways the Fed’s Basel III Pivot Unlocks Institutional Bitcoin Custody". We are 108,687 blocks from the next halving — the mathematical scarcity event that reshapes Bitcoin's economy. Block 941,313 was sealed in the chain today. Every block is irreversible proof that this network has never stopped — not for banks, not for governments, not for anything.

todays_insights: Bitcoin is 44.7% below its ATH of $126,080 — currently in drawdown.
95.25% of all Bitcoin that will ever exist has been mined (20,003,043 BTC out of 21,000,000).
Market sentiment: Extreme Fear (23/100). Historically, extremes signal reversals.
Hash rate stands at 1052.7 EH/s — measuring the total computational security of the network.
Next halving: 108,687 blocks away (~755 days). Block reward will drop from 3.125 to 1.5625 BTC.
Mempool is quiet: fees as low as 1 sat/vByte.
todays_tips: Extreme Fear: This could be a buying opportunity, but only if it aligns with your long-term strategy.
Lower fees mean this is a good time to batch on-chain transactions or use Lightning for smaller payments.
Always do your own research. Bitcoin is about financial sovereignty — learn, stack sats, and HODL.
todays_fact: Bitcoin halvings occur roughly every 4 years and cut the new supply issuance in half, reinforcing scarcity.
todays_prediction: Short-term momentum is neutral, but low Fear & Greed suggests this bear market could still have room. (Not financial advice.)
